summary: TEST card from review of RunPod's new official ComfyUI images (cuda12.8/cuda13.0, ComfyUI 0.26 + torch 2.10) + ComfyUI-RunpodDirect. Neither fixes our aimdo fault-in bug (RunpodDirect = network-fetch; our bug = RAM->VRAM fault, read_bytes=0). BUT their prebuilt cu130 image = free test rig for the ONE axis pod-perf-investigation.md never isolated: cu130/CUDA13/py3.12 environment fault-hook path. Test = deploy runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0 on a 32GB 5090, aria2c-pull the LTX bf16 set from R2, run one gen, read stage-1 fault-in vs the 108-127s baseline. <60s -> cu130 IS the lever (justifies our own cu130 rebuild); still ~120s -> environment cleared, Fix #3 (highvram-resident) is the only lever. Download script written to scratchpad.

LIVE r580-floor confirmed: deployed runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0, landed on an old-driver host (found version 12080 = CUDA 12.8 max) -> torch cu130 RuntimeError NVIDIA driver too old, ComfyUI crashed on boot. Not the image - the HOST driver. Exactly the r580-floor cost pod-perf-investigation.md flagged for cu130. FIX for the test: RunPod console deploy has a CUDA Version filter -> set 13.0 -> only r580+ hosts. IN-CODE GAP FOUND: our createPod spec sends NO allowedCudaVersions (grep runpodRemote.js/remotePodLifecycle.js) -> Pod placement is driver-roulette today. REST POST /pods + GraphQL podFindAndDeployOnDemand BOTH accept allowedCudaVersions:["13.0"] as a hard placement filter (same pattern as minMemoryInGb, MPI-160). Spawned MPI-188 to plumb it. HARD PREREQ if MPI-187 greenlights a cu130 image rebuild.

Pickup: cu130 image LTX fault-in test underway. Download script ready (scratchpad, R2 URLs live-verified); first deploy hit the r580-floor crash (logged) -> re-deploy runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0 with console CUDA=13.0 filter, run script, read stage-1 fault-in vs 108-127s baseline.

*** RESULT: cu130 IS THE LEVER. ~10x fault-in collapse. *** Live on RunPod official runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0, RTX 5090 32GB, driver 580.159, CUDA 13.0, torch 2.10, py3.12, LTX bf16 set. Log: 11:58:04 Requested to load LTXAV -> Model LTXAV 40050MB Staged -> 11:58:18 Model Initialization complete! 11.25s/it = STAGE-1 FAULT-IN ~11-13s (baseline was 108-127s on torch 2.8-2.11/cu126/py3.11 Pods; even beats local ~34s). Full t2v Prompt executed in 49.48s. This ISOLATES the one axis pod-perf-investigation.md could never control (cu130/CUDA13/py3.12/driver-580 UVM fault path) and PROVES it. NOT torch-version (2.11 moved it 0%), NOT VRAM, NOT disk (all previously cleared). FLIPS the docs CONCLUDED-do-not-cu130 verdict (that was about cuBLAS GEMM compute=Blackwell-only; it EXPLICITLY never tested the cu130 fault-hook path -> this did). NEXT: rebuild OUR Pod image on a cu130 base. HARD PREREQ = MPI-188 (allowedCudaVersions guard, r580 floor). Also validated: RunPod official image boots instant + downloads instant (models 402MiB/s aria2 -x16, container-disk no-volume) -> feeds MPI-186. Fold this datapoint into pod-perf-investigation.md elimination table + lift the cu130 dead-end banner.

i2v CONTINUE path confirms cu130 across ALL stages (RTX 5090, cu130). Full multi-stage i2v w/ latent upload: stage-1 re-fault ~26s (21.65s/it init), stage-2 re-fault ~12s, VideoVAE+AudioVAE clean, Prompt executed in 73.96s total. Per-STAGE re-fault (the old no-free-warm-gen curse: ~34-58s on torch2.8-2.11 Pods) is now ~12-26s = ~5x cheaper per fault. So Fix #3 (pin-resident/no-refault) drops from ONLY-hope to optional polish. Every fault-in on this stack is cheap. Evidence chain now: t2v stage-1 ~11s, t2v full 49.48s, i2v full-continue 73.96s. cu130/CUDA13/py3.12/driver-580 confirmed the lever end-to-end, single-stage AND multi-stage. USER CALLED IT (ComfyUI recommends cu130 for new versions) — prior agents killed cu130 on the wrong axis (cuBLAS GEMM=Blackwell-only compute) and never tested the fault-hook path. Verdict flipped.

DECISIVE: aimdo ruled OUT as the variable — the fast Pod runs comfy-aimdo 0.4.10 (SAME version our slow Pods ran) + comfy-kitchen 0.2.10, aimdo ACTIVE (40050MB Staged in logs). So the ~10x is NOT aimdo-absence and NOT aimdo-version. EXACT REFERENCE STACK (RunPod runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0, the ~11s fault-in): torch 2.10.0+cu130, torchvision 0.25.0+cu130, torchaudio 2.10.0+cu130, CUDA 13.0, driver 580.159.03, py3.12, ComfyUI 0.26.2, comfy-aimdo 0.4.10, comfy-kitchen 0.2.10, triton 3.6.0, numpy 2.5.0, transformers 5.12.1, safetensors 0.8.0, kornia 0.8.2, sm_120 (5090 Blackwell). SHARPENED CONCLUSION: NOT the torch minor (2.10 < the 2.11 we tested that gave 0%!) — it is the +cu130 BUILD / CUDA-13 toolkit / driver-580 UVM fault-hook path. torch 2.10+cu130 = ~11s; torch 2.11+cu126 = 126s. The CUDA toolkit generation is the lever, not the pytorch version number. THIS IS THE REBUILD RECIPE — pin our Pod image to this stack. Same aimdo/ComfyUI we already ship; only the torch-CUDA base moves cu126->cu130 + driver floor r580 (=MPI-188).

Test COMPLETE + accepted. cu130 proven the LTX fault-in fix (~10x, 108-127s->~11s), full evidence chain + reference stack captured, pod-perf doc corrected. No work remains on the test itself; the ACTION (rebuild on cu130) lives in the spawned follow-ups: MPI-188 (guard), MPI-186 (image arch), MPI-189 (cu130 rebuild), MPI-190 (bf16 revert).
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
